Hi... Don Crowther here.
As promised, I've got a bunch of killer Social Marketing strategies and tactics in this report. But is it OK
with you if I start this report on something of a personal note?
As I type this, it's been just a little over 24 hours since we released my “No Secrets” video about Social
Marketing.
Now I have to be honest – I knew the video was really good.
After all, it was obvious the content rocked... and with Andy Jenkins working his crazy video skills on it
production side, well, I knew we had a winning combination.
But I simply was not prepared for what happened. Simply put, the world turned upside down in 24
hours. We had 39,219 people go watch the video. We had 152 people leave raving comments on the
Stomper blog. More people are sending email. Blogs are linking to the video. It was a happy madness!
And we have all kinds of people who want to sign up for the SMARTS Coaching Program... even though
we haven't released it yet, and we haven't even told anyone what's in the program.
(If you haven't seen the video yet, go watch it here...)
That was all cool. And very gratifying... after all, I have been working myself to the bone for the last six
months researching this stuff like some mad scientist.
But the really weird thing is that during much of those 24 hours I was huddled in my basement with my
family as a line of tornadoes raged by outside. Hard to believe, but you can't make this stuff up – there
has only been ONE TIME in recorded history when tornadoes hit my home state of Wisconsin in the
month of January.

But it was almost like that video stirred things up, because they hit us with a vengeance. One of them
was on the ground for 20 minutes... and it was just 10 miles from my house. The wind nearly destroyed

So as I sat huddled there in my basement with my family... well, you know what runs through your head.
You think about what's really important. You know what I mean.
Family. Friends. Home. Community. Security. My relationships.
Isn't it amazing how in those kinds of situations our thoughts ALWAYS go to those things? To the things
that are REALLY important?
And then my brain made a bit of a weird shift. Because all those things are EXACTLY what Social Media is
all about.
Because Social Media and Web 2.0 are truly bringing the world closer. We are all getting more and more
connected. We are building relationships with people around the world, and we're doing it at an
amazing pace.
This is a huge tidal wave... and if your business is not positioned in front of that wave, then you're going
to be left behind. This is a FUNDAMENTAL shift on the Internet and even in the wider world.
There are billions of conversations taking place, and many of them are either going to be talking about
your business, or they're going to be talking about your competitor's business.
Which do YOU want to have happen?
(Or even worse, they might be listening to your competitor talk about YOUR business!)
Back To The Future...
I mentioned this in the “No Secrets” video... Internet Marketing is about to change radically. In fact, it's
already changed radically... and your business may very well ALREADY be suffering from these changes.
But there's a silver lining to these changes... in many ways it's making Internet Marketing EASY again.
And I'm going to show you why we are going into a new golden era for your internet business.
You see, I've been doing business online for a long time – since way back in 1997. And I'll tell you this... a
lot of things were easier back then. It was easy to get listed in the search engines. It was easy to get

First Mover – What's The Big Deal?
So just how important is first mover advantage? I have one word for you... Amazon.com. Think about
it... there are an awful lot of places where you can buy books online these days. But what's the first one
that comes to mind? Which one has the largest market share?
Want another example? How about online auctions... there are a LOT of places that have online
auctions. But what's the first one you think of? eBay, right?
Now I'm not claiming that you are going to build the next Amazon.com or eBay, but you CAN have first
mover advantage if you beat your competitors in the Social Marketing game.
Fortunes are being made, right now, by those who are capitalizing on the social media world.
We look back now at the book‐selling business and online auctions and wonder how they got those
massive market shares! And 10 years from now, people are going to be looking at the businesses that
will be built in the next 1‐2 years and shake their head in wonder.
And the best part is that it's EASY... and you get results FAST. This isn't rocket science. It isn't even SEO
(and we all know that can get pretty complicated pretty fast!)
In fact, Social Marketing gives you the benefit of direct traffic from some of the highest trafficked sites in
the world, PLUS a big boost in the search engines, PLUS a new source of responsive prospects.
It's a win win win situation.

Critical Factor #1:
Better Search Rankings
Before we cover specific strategies and tactics, let's talk about some of the important big picture stuff...
and we're going to start with improving your search engine rankings. I said this in the video ‐ if you do it
right, social marketing means that you don’t have to be a rocket scientist to get to the top of Google.
In fact, you can get 2, 3, even 8 or 10 spots on the first page of search engine results for many of your
keywords. So now virtually anyone who searches for those keywords has a huge probability of landing
on one of your pages!
I already showed how our Going Natural 2.0 videos scored top rankings... within HOURS of publishing
our first video, we OWNED the first eight PAGES of Google results. Let me repeat that... I'm not talking
about the first eight results... I am talking about the first eight PAGES. That means we got the top 80
results.
What’s important to realize here is that we did all of this without doing any link building. It all happened
by itself, as a result of successful social marketing.
And the net result is that 139,219 people have watched those videos, there are discussions on blogs and
forums all over the net, and our visibility and positioning has increased substantially in the world of
Internet Marketing.
But, you say, your examples for a made up term that nobody would have been searching on otherwise.
This can’t work in the REAL world of highly competitive keyword terms.
That's just one example. I gave you lots more in the video, including specific stats about highly
competitive key words. For example, the term “beautiful girls,” which WordTracker.com tells us gets
searched for over 366,000 times per month. Eight out of the top ten results on Google and seven of the

The bottom line is that there's no faster way to the top of the search engines than through Social

Critical Factor #2:
Big Direct Traffic
Your social marketing campaign will also drive big traffic DIRECTLY to your site from the social media
sites, and that's huge. After all, that's really the holy grail, isn't it? We all want more traffic to our site,
right?
Well, remember... these social media sites have more traffic than Google. And if you use the right
strategies and tactics to put yourself in front of that traffic, then you don't have rely on Google to get
traffic to your sites. And you don't have to worry about expensive AdWords campaigns, or other
advertising.
Of course, let's be real... just because you start getting big traffic directly from the social media sites
doesn't mean you will abandon your Google rankings and stop all advertising. What it means is that you
will be getting a lot more traffic, and the average cost of that traffic will plummet down to levels that are
more like 1998... there's that “back to the future” stuff I've been talking about.
And here's why we're so excited about social marketing (and our SMARTS technologies)... in this new
frontier, Social MARKETING can spike your traffic FAST, and if you do it right, you get LONG TERM Search
Engine traffic too. It's really the best of both worlds.
I mentioned this example in the video... Photobucket generates 55% of their traffic from their Widgets
and applications on MySpace and Bebo, and it's now replaced Flickr as the largest photo sharing site in
the UK. Why? Because for a variety of reasons Flickr didn't properly use social media and only gets 2.4%
of its traffic from social media sites. What a great example of "you snooze you lose!"

10 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
TRUST you before they even hit your web site. It's not rocket science.
Consider the recent test performed by MarketingExperiments.com, a site that runs online tests and
records the results in their newsletters. They recently concluded a 12‐month test where they measured
the results of social marketing versus buying paid traffic to a series of 4 sites. Their results were quite
compelling. Using social marketing, they were able to generate over 93,000 visitors to their sites, paying
an employee to do the social marketing work for a total of $3600 during that time, resulting in a total
cost per visitor of 4 cents. Then they ran a one month test driving traffic from Google AdWords. Using
that strategy, they obtained 2,057 visitors after investing $1,250 in click costs, resulting in a cost per
visitor of 61 cents. Four 4 cents versus 61 cents ‐ social marketing yielded a 1,427% better return on
their investment. Not bad!

Strategy #1:
Place content on HubPages.com
Remember in the video where we discussed the advantages of creating Squidoo lenses and putting
content on Scribd.com?
Hubpages.com is another great example of a site where you can post content, get it read by others, and
get people to visit your site.
HubPages is basically an article publishing site, where you can set up an account, post articles or other
content (hey, there’s even some poems up on this site!) and get great, quick search engine exposure for
your pages.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 42 --

But there are some other factors that make HubPages particularly interesting.
First, at least at this moment, it doesn’t nofollow links. So links, carefully and judiciously embedded, pass
link value to your site.
Second, HubPages allows you to post sidebars above the fold and in other locations on your article.
Some Hubbers use that space for a picture, like
this:
But others use them to post marketing copy and links
that drive people to their pages like this:
One advantage that HubPages has over Squidoo is that could take less effort to build out a HubPage
than a Squidoo lens, so you get a better ROI.
Another is that, while HubPages allows you to monetize your site like Squidoo, HubPages gives you a
much bigger percentage of the revenues than Squidoo (60% of all the revenues you generate – they do
this by showing your ad 60% of the time, and theirs 40%). That means that HubPages could result in a
nice little check arriving in your account each month, in addition to the revenues you earn from getting
more people to come to your site.
So, you can just do quick and dirty pages, or you can take things another step further, by putting a little
more work into your pages to both add interest, and to try to become one of HubPages “featured
pages” which will drive even more traffic to your pages.
So you can add:

• 	Pictures
• 	Videos

• 	Links to other HubPages
• 	Links to other sites

Now strategic Social Marketers probably got pretty excited as they read that list because they saw
opportunities to piggyback options on their HubPages. For example, you could:
• 	Link out to your other related sites
While this is obvious, it needs to be included in this list
• 	Embed your own, related YouTube videos into your HubPage.
While this won’t earn you any more money, views of your embedded YouTube video contribute
to moving your video up in the search results on YouTube, giving you even more views there.
• 	Embed your related RSS feeds.
If you have a blog or other RSS feed which contains related content, you can embed that feed
into your HubPage, giving you more traffic to your blog AND generating incoming links
That’s one way to kick your HubPages up to a higher level (and make more direct and indirect revenue in
the process,) but there’s another way too – you can create a Flagship Hub. The goal of a flagship Hub is
to be the go‐to guide on a subject. This means that it needs to be more than just a quick and easy article
slapped into HubPages. You put more work into it, so that people will say “this is the best page I’ve ever
seen on the subject.”
Plus they actually pay you to produce one. They don’t pay much, just $25‐$35 extra dollars, in addition
to the 60% split on ad revenues you’ll get from the page. Plus, they’ll even give you an extra bonus if you
get links from other sites and generate at least 10 visitors from 10 other pages, so they’re clearly
wanting these pages to get great rankings in the search engines.
And presumably, they’re going to feature your page higher on their site, because you’ve created a go‐to
page on the subject.
There IS a set of minimum standards for this. A flagship page must have
• 	A minimum of 1500 words, of your own, purely original writing
• 	A minimum of 3 relevant video capsules (YouTube or Google)
• 	A minimum of 5 relevant non‐copyrighted images
• 	At least 10 relevant, helpful links to other sources, each with your own 30‐50 word description
• 	A news capsule (or highly‐relevant RSS feed), or eBay Capsule if your topic is product‐related
• 	15 relevant tags, including 2‐3 common misspellings or spelling variants (if any)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 42 --

• 	Maximum 150 word succinct description of the utility of your Flagship Hub in the Summary Text
(uncheck the Calculate Summary Automatically box)
• 	Excellent thematic and aesthetic organization ‐ use the Capsule Ordering setup to the right (or
something similar) when laying out your Hub—trust us, it will look great when you enter all your
content
Additional rules:
• 	The content must not be copied from any other source, even if you
have the rights to copy from that source. It must be 100% original,
and not copied elsewhere after publication and payment, either.
• 	You should include all links in the Links Capsule, and the links
should not be to “bad neighborhoods” (sketchy sites with adult,
gambling or other questionable content or ads) or to affiliate sites
• 	Please break up your text into sections (using the Heading 2 text
treatment for each section title) so that it is easy to read.
You will have to sign a contract, and have the HubPage done within 7 days
of contract.
(Source and for more information:
http://hubpages.com/help/flagship_hubs_more)

While flagship Hubs clearly take more work, HubPages will undoubtedly feature them, and be an even
stronger source of traffic for you, so they are probably worth spending the extra time to complete.
Here are a couple of ways to use flagship hubs in your social marketing:
1. 	Very early in each month, scan the Flagship Hubs Topic Pool at
http://hubpages.com/help/flagship_hubs_pool to see if it contains anything related to your
topic areas. If it does, you can snatch it up by contracting to produce it. Then place links to your
site into the page you produce, pick up your videos, etc. so that part of your flagship page
references your existing site(s)
2. 	On a regular basis, complete Flagship Hub requests that are from topic areas on your list of
possible future sites. This will not only give you experience in these areas, but should also give
you indications of traffic and revenues associated with these topic areas
And here are a few ideas on how to work HubPages into your Social Marketing plan:
1. 	Develop HubPages in each of the major topic areas for your subject matter, linking into your
regular site(s)

Strategy #2:
Get Instant Demographically‐Targeted Traffic From
Facebook.com
(If you’re an AdWords advertiser, you’re going to love this one.)
Have you ever wished there was a way that you could target very specific audiences with online
promotions?
For example, college graduates. Or knitters, or runners, or people who are engaged, or people who like
hip hop music?
Or, to even get more granular than that, how about graduates from Ohio State University?
I can tell you, it’s hard to do.
And even if you succeed, the targeting is dubious at best (how do they know that person actually fits
that demographic, or are they just modeling it, and the cost to get such an audience is incredibly
expensive.
You can target search behavior, by running pay per click on the search engines. But that only works if
they are actively searching for that specific topic area at the time that you are
• 	Running your ad
• 	Have a high enough budget set for that day so that your ad actually shows up (remember that if
your daily budget is less than the amount they forecast you should be budgeting, your ad only
shows up a fraction of the times it could that day.)
• 	Are bidding a high enough amount for that click that they actually SEE your ad because it's not
buried deep on another page
• 	Choose the right keywords so your ad CAN appear
And all of that still doesn’t give you any kind of demographic targeting!
So, is there a solution to this problem?
Actually, there is.
It’s Facebook.
Now we could talk about creating a Facebook Page, which you should do, and joining Facebook groups,
which you should also do. And you should probably start a group yourself.
But all of those take time to really pan out. In the meantime, we want instant traffic and sales.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 42 --

Here’s what I do. I run Facebook Ads.
Facebook ads run on the user’s profile when they come to their page.
So they see your ad when they log in to check their Facebook profile.
But what’s better than that is that you can buy very tight demographics. And those demographics aren’t
created by guessing or computer modeling (the way these things are normally done), but they’re
defined by the member themselves!
The member says where they graduated from, their age, marital status.
Then the member defines their interests, their favorite books, music, and movies.
So, now Facebook gives you the ability to advertise in a way that simply isn’t available anywhere else.
And best of all, it’s affordable. You don’t have to spend tens of thousands of dollars per month, and you
don’t have to spend tons per click.
Technically, you can buy the ads for just a few cents apiece, but I’ve found that a minimum bid of 20
cents is really necessary to get your ad shown.
Your ads can appear either in the context of the page or on the left side.
And another thing that gives them power is that you can also include an image in your ad, drawing more
attention to it, and giving you yet another copy opportunity.
Here are a few examples of ads that are running on Facebook right now to give you ideas and a flavor
for the types of ads Facebook users are seeing:
17 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 42 --

18 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 42 --

Notice that you’re seeing everything from leasing a Lexus and finding reliable contractors to picking up
women and t‐shirts. There’s a wide range of items that are being sold (presumably successfully) on
Facebook.
The ad creation process is simple (though you have to be careful not to get too salesy, or your ad will get
rejected). These ads can be used to advertise either a regular web page or a Facebook Page, so you can
test whether your ad is more effective staying on Facebook or going directly to your site.
The targeting can be nationally, by State/Province or by City. You can choose sex, age, education status
(all, college graduate, in college, and in high school), workplaces, political views (liberal, moderate,
conservative), or by relationship status (single, in a relationship, engaged or married). Finally, you can
target any keywords they used on their page, like interests, favorite music, movies, etc.)
Imagine the power!
And it’s all pay per click, so you only pay for the ads that actually get clicked on.
Think about this – powerful targeting, pennies per click, good analytics and opportunities for growth as
Facebook grows in the future.
Consider using Facebook Ads to:
• 	Advertise your website
• 	Advertise your link bait (hoping that people will see it and start word of mouth about it)
• 	Advertise your Facebook Pages to get more fans for them and increase the power of these tools
19 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0

Strategy #3:
Utilize the Power of StumbleUpon to Drive Traffic to Your Site
StumbleUpon is an amazing site in terms of driving traffic. Basically, people sign up for StumbleUpon,
they indicate which topic areas they are interested in, then load a toolbar and vote on which sites they
like and which ones they don’t.
But where it really gets interesting is that members can, at any time, click on a Stumble button and be
taken to a page that fits their areas of interest. In fact, so many people do that StumbleUpon has
become a major traffic source for many sites, especially those that have some kind of interesting or
entertaining linkbait associated with them.
For example our data for www.StomperBlog.com shows that, other than direct and internal traffic, the
number 2 source of traffic for that site is StumbleUpon!
And that we actually get more traffic to that site from StumbleUpon than from Google!!!
Hmmm... maybe this IS worth paying attention to.
And that traffic isn’t worthless either. Our research has shown that it buys and clicks on ads at levels at
rates that are usually at least equal and sometimes higher than other sites.
So, here’s what’s happening for your site right now, whether you’re a member of this service or not
• 	StumbleUpon members who come to your site are seeing it, and rating it either up or down
• 	They’re also adding reviews
• 	Some may be sharing your site with friends
• 	If your site is getting good ratings, StumbleUpon is including it in their list of sites that they feed
out to people who click the StumbleUpon button. So you’re getting free traffic, with no effort on
your part!
Want to get more of that traffic? We’ll show you how in a minute.
But first, it’s probably a good idea for you to see how your site is currently ranking with StumbleUpon
members.
To do so, join as a member and install the toolbar. Then go to your site.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 42 --

You can choose either direction, but if it were me, I’d probably click the thumbs up “I like it!” button
After you do that, go to the toolbar and click the 	button. You’ll see a result like this:
See where it says “Show me more (35)”? That presumably means that 35 Stumblers have rated this site
with a thumbs up.
Then drop down to where you see…
You’ll probably find the reviews to be very interesting, because here’s where you see reviews from both
people who both like and dislike it.
You may want to review your own site – I have no idea what you might say about it :<)
Make sure that you tag it appropriately in this process. Lots of tags are good.
From now on, you won’t be able to rate or comment on it again, though I have found that you can
sometimes add tags later on.
So, that’s it. Rate your site, then cross your fingers and hope that you get lots of good ratings and more
free traffic from StumbleUpon. Next strategy…
21 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 42 --

Or is that really it?
What if there was a way to quickly get lots of Stumblers to see your site and hopefully rate it higher?
There is!
You’ll find it on the bottom of StumbleUpon.com under the link for Advertisers.
There you’ll find the ability to “Create a campaign now.”
This button basically allows you to buy Stumbles for just a nickel apiece. You’ll be asked to fund the
advertising account for just $25 from your Paypal account, which will then buy you 500 Stumblers seeing
your site. Then they’ll have the ability to rate your site either with a thumbs up or down.
You can even control the exposures so that you are targeting campaigns by Category (with over 500 to
choose from, Location (country, state, and city), and Demographics (age and gender.) One warning, they
don’t have this information for the majority of their Stumblers, so choosing any kind of targeting will
significantly increase the amount of time it takes to get that first 500 Stumblers.
Hopefully, your site will be of a quality that the majority of people who see it will rate it with a thumbs
up and will convert into buying traffic. If that’s the case you may want to create an ongoing campaign,
because, after all, you can’t get traffic at a nickel a click from most places.
And remember, you’re not just paying for that traffic. Your purchased clicks that result in thumbs up
votes translate into more free Stumbled traffic in the future, possibly for years.
Now, let me make a point here. Just because YOU think you have a cool site, doesn’t mean that
Stumblers will. They tend to vote well for sites that are interesting, unique and entertaining. So, if you’ve
got a plain old boring site, you may not do too well in this voting. I recommend that you invest $25, see
what kinds of votes you get, then make a decision to go further.
22 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 42 --

But what happens if you get a bad rating?
Well, you could just give up, and go onto the next strategy.
Or, you could work to come up with something the does qualify as being interesting, unique and
entertaining. In other words, you’re going to need
to come up with some quality link bait. Then you
will steer your StumbleUpon traffic to the link
bait’s URL, assuming it isn’t your new homepage. 	Note:
In preparing this report, I debated for quite
awhile whether I should break out creating link
bait as a separate strategy.
It’s important to recognize the true power of
link bait in Social Marketing. People like to chat
about what is popular, and a successful link
bait campaign is designed to do just that.
Don’t underestimate the power of these types
of campaigns in your Social Marketing Plan. A
great link bait campaign could make it so you
don’t have to actually do anything else in your
Social Marketing campaign, because other
people will do that work for you!
That’s a report in and of itself, and we’ll be
discussing it in lots more detail as part of our
coaching course. But think about successful
campaigns that you’ve heard of like Subservient
chicken and Elf Yourself (which was turned off
after the holiday).
Some ideas for link bait and viral promotion
include developing:
• 	Hot videos
• 	Lists
• 	Quizzes
• 	Tests
• 	Interactive video
• 	Unique images
• 	Tools
• 	Things that teach how to do something
So, if you aren’t getting the ratings you think you should, don’t give up, just change your offer so that
you get the ratings you want. It’ll not only help your StumbleUpon ranking, but everything else you do.
There’s one more thing you may find interesting in using StumbleUpon. They have a tool, which you can
install on your browser, which puts StumbleUpon ratings on search results on Google, etc. This is a great
way to instantly see which sites in yours and other categories, which helps to generate insights into how
you can change your site to increase your rankings.
Strategy #4:
Let Other People Create Video For YOU!
23 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 42 --

24 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
In our movie we discussed the power and importance of creating video to use as a Social Marketing tool
and how to do it at a very inexpensive price.
Well, there’s an even cheaper way to do video, that’s actually far less time consuming and potentially
even more powerful as a Social Marketing tool…
Get other people to create video for you!
Yes, you read that correctly.
I’ve always been a huge fan of not working whenever possible. And my years of focusing on that has
generated a ton of ideas. But getting other people to create your videos for you is one of the very best.
Basically, here’s the way it works.
You create some kind of contest, awarding people prizes for creating video about your product or the
subject area that you’re covering.
Make the prize big enough and worthwhile enough to truly catch people’s interest and make them
actually want to do it.
Now, I’ll tell you, this works the best when there’s something catchy or interesting in the subject area
this video is about.
One of my very favorite examples of this is the cough medicine that apparently works, but tastes
horrible, so they did a contest for people to show the face they make after taking the medicine.
Or, there’s NesQuik’s “How do you shake it” contest
Or Heinz Ketchup’s commercial contest
You can see a list of contests currently running on YouTube at http://youtube.com/contests_main
The key is that your contest needs to either have a very entertaining element to it, or it needs to have a
prize that’s big enough that people are willing to work on it even though it may not be as exciting. There
are lots of things that people will do for fame and fortune!
Another key is that you structure the contest so that people logically include your product as the hero in
the contest. You probably don’t want to just do an off‐the‐wall contest. You want something where your
product solves their problem.
That way the videos that get produced actually become commercials for your product.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 42 --

25 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
I also recommend that you run the contest through YouTube, so that the entries are posted directly on
YouTube or some other video site. That way people are adding lots of videos onto YouTube that contain
your product as the hero, generating more traffic and search results.
Now, I caution you on this, because most people naturally will want to have the videos submitted to
them. But that defeats part of the purpose of this whole promotion.
Remember, you’re not just trying to get a video in the end, you’re trying to get lots of people talking
about the contest and more importantly, the videos that get produced.
Ideally, you’ll have 3‐5 different videos that have viral power all their own.
One way to do that is to make number of views and comments be part of the judging process. Don’t
make it the sole judging process though, because there are some people who will cheat and do
automated video viewing and comments.
Ideally what you’re hoping for is that those who enter the contest will get all of their friends to come
and view their videos, who will then get all of their friends, etc. until you’ve got tons of people viewing
the videos and hopefully coming to your site as a result.
Of course, you’ll want to own all rights to the winners, and to post all of the entrants on your site in the
process of the site and even afterwards.
Some people do video contests that invite people to give video testimonials. Rich Schefren just finished
his – and got 89 videos in response. You can see them at http://www.strategicprofits.com/blog/we‐
have‐our‐winners
Part of his judging was the number of Diggs received for each one. That’s a great idea too! Anything that
helps turn the contest from your site to the social media helps.
Want to kick this strategy up another notch?
What if you were to work with groups that have people who want to shoot video and make money from
it? College and high school video classes come to mind.
Imagine the power of getting 50 different college professors to make entering your contest an
assignment for their class. Now that will take some longer timeframes, some extra marketing, and a
prize for the department at the University where the entry came from.
Can you see the potential here?
Or, what if we take this whole “get other people to do video for you” idea another step further. This
time, instead of doing a contest, what if you paid people to produce videos for you? Remember the
HubPages Flagship Hubs? What if you paid people to produce videos on the topics you assign to them.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

26 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
Basically, get 5 or so people to do it for each topic. Then have extra bonuses for the best results. Maybe
you could even have your site and social network visitors vote on the results.
And, of course, all of this would be supported by announcements in the social networks, pages in the
content sharing sites, bookmarks, and your OWN killer videos announcing this killer process or content.

Strategy #5:
Incorporate video into your actual sales process
Many people think of online video as an entertainment and possibly a lead generation tool. And it’s
great for that.
But never forget what it’s best at – selling stuff.
Come on, nobody ever does that, right?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 42 --

27 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
Actually that’s not true. Video gets used thousands of times EVERY DAY to sell stuff.
We call them “commercials” and they show up every 8 minutes on your TV.
So why aren’t more people using video commercials as part of their selling process?
I don’t know, I’m clueless. That’s why I’m including this in this report.
We’re seeing some amazing results when people include video on their sales pages.
We’ve seen 15%, even 40% increases in sales simply by including a video on the sales page.
Now, I have to also state that we’ve also seen video decrease sales, especially when the video loads
slowly, and/or replaces the image of the actual product on the sales page. So, video in and of itself is not
the magic button. You still have to do it correctly. Testing is vital. But when you get all of the pieces
correctly into place, it rocks!
So let’s see, how could you use online video to shift from lead generation (the SMARTS video you
recently watched was a lead generation video) to actually selling product? Here are some ideas:
• 	Create a video to run on your home page that talks about why they should buy from you
rather than your competitors. Here www.MyWeddingFavors.com does that by using a popup
video.
• 	Create separate Home Shopping Network type videos that feature the product visually,
making it look VERY appealing (Here’s an example that resulted in a 35% increase in sales, just
by adding that video.)
• 	Create a side‐by‐side demo video that demonstrates your product’s strengths versus your
competitor’s (Here’s an example of one I recently did using that strategy. It’s Camtasia, and
should be actual video, but it was all I had time for at the moment.)
• 	Do a video that’s actually a press release, or use it to supplement a press release. (Here’s
another example using that strategy.)
• 	Do a video to improve your opt‐in confirmation, showing exactly what to expect in the opt‐in
process. (Here’s an example from AWeber.com, my favorite autoresponder company)
• 	Create lessons on how to use your product (here’s a case study from AWeber.com showing
how one company’s integrated their online video with an optin page.)
So why am I including this conversion increase information in a report on Social Marketing?
Because just about every video that you create to increase conversion on your site can also be used in the
social media world!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 42 --

28 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
But, people don’t want to watch essentially commercials or infomercials, right?
Just tell that to Home Shopping Network, which has 77,239 different views of their channel on YouTube
just within the last year. And they have 701 people who have subscribed to their channel, asking to
receive notification of the posting of new video commercials every time a new one is released.
Think about it this way – people are oftentimes reluctant to come to someone’s website, thinking they’ll
be “sold” when they arrive. But they’re still curious, and wanting to know more. So they’re willing to
watch a video on the subject, on the “neutral” ground of the social media or video site.
So your selling message gets conveyed, no matter what site they’re on.
This is a powerful concept that you really ought to be including in your 2008 marketing plan!
Finally, one other piece of evidence that video works to bring people to websites – you’re here, right!
And you probably came because you watched our video!

after all. We REALLY like to overdeliver!
Strategy #6:
Improve your responsiveness to your community by using
Facebook’s toolbar
We’ve already established that you and I aren’t normal. We can’t be, we’ve got far too much going on in
our lives.
But let me tell you about how many people actually include Facebook or MySpace in their daily lives.
They get up. Then they check their Facebook profile to see what happened in the lives of their friends
last night. They may then make a post on their friends' walls or message some of those friends.
Then they go jump in the shower and get dressed.
Then they check their Facebook page again to see if anyone else has replied to their replies or maybe
got up later than them.
Then they head off to start their day.
Now, here’s where things get really interesting. Remember this is a social media. Sociality thrives on
conversation and quick response.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 42 --

This is probably a good spot to consider the
role social network “friends” play in Social
Marketing campaigns. For “normal” people,
having a huge friends list either means they’re
very social, or very vain. But again, we’re not
normal. For us, a larger group of friends means
that we’ve increased our sphere of influence,
potentially making everything we do from a
Social Marketing perspective even more
effective.
So, for us, having a huge friends list is not only
a good thing, it’s imperative!
That makes the Facebook toolbar even more
important, because it cuts down the time
required to achieve your online goals.
That’s why it’s valuable for you to have a way to
quickly see the conversation among your group
of friends.
And if there is an element of that conversation
that you can contribute to, you can do it quickly,
and while they are likely still on the site to see it.
Which improves your perception of being on the
ball, accurate and helpful – building an even
deeper bond of trust.
That’s where the Facebook Toolbar comes into
play. It’s a toolbar that runs as part of your

US/firefox/addon/3794 and for Internet Explorer

acebook.php

In it you get the ability to quickly search Facebook for new friends (just got an email from someone not
in your friends group? This makes it easy to add them immediately.)
There’s a sidebar, where you can check, with one click, the current status of everyone on your friends
list. But more importantly, you can use it to quickly and easily message anyone on your list. In fact, as I
made the screenshot below, I realized that I needed to say something to Paul, so I clicked on the
message link, wrote him a 30 second message that only he will see, and sent it off to him. No need to
open email, or hope it doesn’t get caught in his spam filters, etc. Very convenient!

(Special update, it’s now 24 minutes since I wrote the statement above. Paul is at CES, a huge computer

There’s also a set of indicators that show how many messages you have on the Facebook system so you

Oh, and there’s also a very cool share button that makes it easy to share a web page or blog post that

But the thing that I find most powerful is the Facebook alerts. With this feature turned on, I get a little
popup in the corner of my screen that shows me when any of my friends change their status. Most of
them are only mildly interesting, but some of them contain something which I can react to and add to
the conversation.
Let me give you an example of how this works in a real world situation.
As we launched the SMARTS video I was as nervous as all get out. Would anyone actually watch it?
Would they think it was any good?
And suddenly, just an hour after the launch, I saw a popup message on my screen. One of my Facebook
friends, Scott Kublin posted: “Just finished watching Don Crowther's world's coolest video on the power
of social media.”
I’ve gotta tell you, that made my day. Not only did he like it, but the timing of his post meant that he
started watching it minutes after it released and posted right after that.
So I immediately jumped over to his wall and made a thank you post to Scott. Total elapsed time, from
his post to my reply – 5 minutes, only 2 minutes of that spent actually typing the post.
Now, Scott and I aren’t personal friends, though I hear that he’s a very cool guy. And I didn’t do anything
here that instantly sold him anything or even steered him back into one of my webpages (hey, he’d just
finished watching my video for almost an hour, what more could I ask from him at this moment!)
30 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

But I can guarantee that the next time I see him, we’ll have a much deeper conversation. His post, my

That’s the power of social networking, made even easier by tools available like Facebook toolbar.
Can You Tell I’m Passionate About This Stuff…
We’re Not Done Yet!
OK, we've come a long way. I promised you a 33 page report... and it's now pretty obvious I 'm going to
run more than a little long on that estimate. And that's after that 53 minute video I gave you a couple of
days ago...
So let's catch our breath and look at how far we've come...
I showed you the Social Marketing opportunity and how this is your last best chance at “first mover”
advantage... and how this represents the biggest online opportunity since way back when we were just
getting ecommerce figured out in the late 90's. It was brain‐dead simple to start a profitable business
back then ‐ and with these huge Web 2.0 changes, well it's almost that simple all over again.
Then I showed you exactly where all the traffic is going on the Internet these days – how the traffic on
these Social Media sites is ALREADY huge (they completely dwarf Google's traffic), and how that traffic is
growing exponentially. If you want to put yourself in front of the big traffic, you need to be using Social
Marketing.
After that I showed you the three pillars of Social Marketing –
1. 	How you can get traffic directly from the Social Media sites... and how you can get it FAST
2. 	How the Social Media sites gives you a near instant boost in the search engines
3. 	How the traffic you get from Social Media sites can be great traffic. Traffic that converts higher
than your traffic from other sources...
That's sorta like the golden triangle of Internet marketing.
And it's a recipe for building your business fast.
And THAT was all just the introduction!
After that I gave you six separate strategies (and that's on top of the four I already gave you in the “No
Secrets” video). As you saw, they aren't rocket science. Virtually anyone can use them in their business
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

32 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
and you can see results very quickly.
Of course, these are just the tip of the iceberg in our overall SMARTS system, but they should be more

Now I have one more thing for you, and it's a biggie...
The ONE Key Thing You Have To Get Right...
This one's a little bit tough to talk about. Because I really don't want to scare you off...
Because if I what I'm about to tell you scares you away from Social Marketing, well that might literally be
the difference between the life and death of your business.
So please read this carefully. Take it as a word of caution, not as a reason to avoid Social Marketing...
because that would be a tragedy.
This is the deal – you NEED to understand the underlying fundamentals in the Social Media world.
People are gathering to communicate with each other. They are gathering to build community.
They are NOT gathering to become prospects for your product.
And if you treat them like they are, then you will destroy the one thing that's irreplaceable...
Your reputation.
No... I'm not getting all warm and fuzzy on you. The bottom line is that your reputation has never been
more important than it is now – because in the new Web 2.0 world, everyone is a publisher. And if you
screw up, the world will hear about it.
Actually, the world will tell each other about it, and then spend a few more days dissecting your fall
from grace.
Take my word on this... as I spent the last six months in deep underground research, I will admit that I
pushed the envelope a few times. Hey, it was all for the sake of research... and I took more than a few
arrows in my back.
Getting your Social Marketing logins cancelled and your site blackballed is NOT very pleasant. But what
really hurts is the damage to your reputation in the marketplace. And that damage can last for YEARS.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

33 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
Thankfully, I was working deep undercover and only using test sites, so it didn't hurt too badly.
But it was still pretty scary. This stuff is powerful, but if you don't use it the right way it can completely

But it's just like anywhere else, if you keep your nose clean and treat people well... then other people
will fall all over themselves to help you out. That's when Social Marketing really comes into its own. And
that's where you want to be...
It's your turn...
OK, it's time to wind this down. I sincerely hope that I've gotten you fired up and ready to implement
Social Marketing in your business. That's what my “No Secrets” video and this Strategy Guide were
designed to do.
As I mentioned, I spent the last six months in deep underground research and in developing our entire
integrated SMARTS technologies. It's been tough, grueling work... but it’s been an amazing experience. I
have conducted what is probably the single most complete and scientific exploration of Social Media
that's ever been done...
I'm talking about conducting hundreds of experiments, thousands of searches, read probably ten
thousand pages of material…
You see, there's something you have to understand about me ‐ there’s a piece of me who’s a scientist
and a statistician. I’m not a seat‐of‐the‐pants, anecdotal kind of guy. I like to find out the facts, to
understand the data and what it’s telling us about what REALLY works, and what doesn’t.
Along the way I’ve had some incredible successes – both in my own projects and some projects I’ve
done for clients. Unfortunately my confidentiality agreements prohibit my sharing the specifics of those
projects with you, but the thing I can share is the techniques we used to achieve that phenomenal
success.
But remember how it took Thomas Edison 10,000 experiments to get the light bulb right? Well, I had
plenty of failures along the way. And that's the key... my “secret strategies” don’t come from stories,
they come from hard facts, from research, and from evidence of what actually works in generating
traffic and what doesn’t.
It's been a lot of work and a very long road. But now I'm ready for the big payoff...

34 SMARTS Strategy Guide: Life and Death 2.0
The bottom line ‐ I know what works, and what doesn’t.
But You Don't Need To Do 10,000 Tests...
Of course, I'm sorta weird... most people don't have that “mad scientist” gene that I seem to have. In
fact, they would much rather go down a tried‐and‐true, proven road then work through a thousand‐and‐
one crazy experiments. Sometimes I wish I could kick the “mad scientist” habit and just be like a regular
person...
Because most folks want a path that they KNOW is going to work. And they want a guide to keep them
on that path. And truth be told... that's the smart way to go – it's pretty much always cheaper AND more
profitable. That's probably the path that you tend to follow.
But I do have a really the big payoff coming... because in addition to that mad scientist part of my
personality, I also LOVE teaching people. You probably got that from my “No Secrets” video, and from
this Strategy Guide, right?
There is nothing more satisfying for me than working with business owners and entrepreneurs, and
seeing them GET RESULTS.
So I just can't wait to get this SMARTS Coaching Program going... the bottom line is that I'm going to get
to work with a limited number of entrepreneurs in an interactive environment for 10 weeks – and I have
so many of these crazy‐good tactics to teach that I can't even count all of them. AND... and I've got the
overarching SMARTS strategy to tie all those tactics together.
This is SWEET stuff!!
So this is how the Coaching Program is going to work...
Every Monday morning I'm going to release a set of online videos on our special members‐only website.
Each week we will have a different module that contains a number of different videos, each covering a
specific strategy (some might give a big picture strategy, some will be extremely specific.)
Along with the videos will be a set of optional assignments. I did say “optional”... but I'm going to be
encouraging you to complete them while the information is fresh in your brain – that's the way you will
get the best results. Of course, we can all get busy at times, so if you just have too much going on... well,
I've got your back.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Then midweek, we'll have a conference call where we go even deeper on that week's topic... and I will
answer your questions. The beauty of these calls is that you learn both from your questions AND the
questions from the other coaching students. These calls will be scheduled for 90 minutes, but I'll
basically stick around until we’re done with all of the questions.
Of course, all calls will be recorded, so if you can’t make one, you can still catch up. Plus, you’ll be able to
submit questions via email in advance, again, in case you can’t be there on the call.
But that’s not all... you will also have an online forum – you can ask questions, get answers, share
experiences, request feedback, and share your insights with the group. Since we’ve already been sharing
many of these videos with the StomperNet members, many of those forums are already populated with
lots of good questions and answers, so there’s ALREADY a big knowledge base just waiting for you!
I also have some more surprises... like calls with some of the top subject‐matter experts in the world on
these subjects. We’re still finalizing the details of who those calls will be with, but I can guarantee, I've
got some very “big” names lined up.
Plus, I make videos every day. So, if questions can best be answered in video or audio form, then I’ll
record one and post it to the site quickly. I'm going to be there as your coach and mentor. If it takes
another video to really crystallize a lesson... then I will make a video for that lesson. It's that simple.
Oh, and I almost forgot the software! We’ve got several very cool tools that will significantly reduce the

going to make all of this stuff as easy as pie.
